Glooko Testing - Follow up Task
tasks.note:
Subject: Discussion on Glooko Cloud Testing Scope, Responsibilities, and Collaboration Framework
Date: [Insert Date]
Attendees: [Insert Attendees]
Prepared By: [Insert Name]
1. Purpose of Meeting
To discuss the scope of end-to-end (E2E) testing involving Glooko Cloud, define responsibilities, establish collaboration mechanisms, and identify information required from Glooko.

2. Discussion Summary
2.1 End-to-End Testing Scope
	• The testing approach should be similar to the validation performed for the mylife App and mylife Cloud, covering the complete user journey.
	• A defined level of E2E testing is expected to ensure that critical workflows function correctly across integrated systems.
	• The scope should be limited to areas that fall within our responsibility and influence.
2.2 Defect Management
	• Glooko should acknowledge and support a defect management process whereby:
		○ Issues identified during testing are communicated to Glooko.
		○ Defects are investigated and fixed within an agreed timeframe.
		○ Progress and resolution status are transparently communicated.
2.3 Testing Approach Clarification
	• Clarification is required from Glooko regarding:
		○ Existing manual testing activities.
		○ Existing automated testing coverage.
		○ Testing performed prior to releases.
	• Understanding their testing strategy will help avoid duplication of effort.
2.4 Staging/Test Environment
	• A staging environment should be made available where integration and verification testing can be executed.
	• Access requirements, environment limitations, and test data management need to be clarified.
2.5 Collaboration Model
	• Establish a dedicated Design Working Group involving representatives from both organizations.
	• Define clear roles and responsibilities for:
		○ Testing activities
		○ Defect management
		○ Change management
		○ Release coordination
	• Regular meetings should be scheduled to ensure alignment and efficient communication.
2.6 Test Plan
	• A detailed test plan should be prepared and released.
	• The plan should include:
		○ Scope and objectives
		○ Test environments
		○ Test responsibilities
		○ Entry and exit criteria
		○ Defect handling process
		○ Deliverables and reporting
2.7 Training
	• Glooko should provide training sessions covering:
		○ Cloud architecture overview
		○ Available features and functionalities
		○ User workflows
		○ Relevant administrative capabilities
2.8 Industry Best Practices
	• Open discussion with Glooko regarding:
		○ How similar collaborations are managed with other device manufacturers.
		○ Recommended best practices for cloud integration testing and verification.
	• Gather lessons learned and successful approaches from existing partnerships.
2.9 Infrastructure and Data Hosting
	• Clarification required regarding:
		○ Server hosting in Ireland.
		○ Whether the Irish infrastructure serves all countries or only specific regions (e.g., Spain).
		○ Availability of separate regional data servers.
		○ Data residency and deployment architecture.
2.10 API and Interface Information
	• Clarify whether there are differences in API endpoints between regions or environments.
	• Current understanding is that there may be no differences, but confirmation is required.
2.11 Documentation Required from Glooko
	• Request confirmation on available documentation, including:
		○ Requirements specifications
		○ Functional specifications
		○ Interface/API specifications
		○ User documentation / IFU (Instructions for Use)
	• Functional specification documents may potentially be shared by Chris.

3. Action Items
#	Action Item	Owner	Due Date
1	Define proposed E2E testing scope and responsibilities.	Internal Team	TBD
2	Request details of Glooko manual and automated testing processes.	Internal Team / Glooko	TBD
3	Clarify availability and access to staging environment.	Glooko	TBD
4	Establish Design Working Group and define roles/responsibilities.	Both Parties	TBD
5	Prepare and release detailed test plan.	Both Parties	TBD
6	Organize Glooko Cloud training sessions.	Glooko	TBD
7	Gather best practices from Glooko's collaborations with other manufacturers.	Glooko	TBD
8	Clarify server architecture, data residency, and regional deployments.	Glooko	TBD
9	Confirm API endpoint strategy across regions and environments.	Glooko	TBD
10	Request requirements, functional specifications, and interface documentation.	Internal Team / Glooko	TBD
4. Next Steps
	• Regroup internally to consolidate expectations and proposed testing responsibilities.
	• Initiate an open discussion with Glooko covering testing strategy, documentation, infrastructure, and collaboration model.
	• Review received information and refine the verification approach accordingly.

Follow up task
